commit.message:
fix(jetson): auto-fill Supabase credentials from repo .env files

Step 8 now reads SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY from repo root
.env or .env.development (committed, public keys) before falling back
to interactive prompt. This means a fresh clone + sudo ./setup_jetson.sh
works without any manual credential entry.

Priority order:
1. Already in config.json (sync_env.py ran on dev machine)
2. Auto-detect from repo .env / .env.development (new)
3. Environment variables (CI/CD)
4. Interactive prompt (provisioning token / manual / skip)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: sprint wrap — vim, tmux, copilot-cli, va alias, CRLF fix (#91)

* fix(copilot-cli): remove conflicting gh alias before extension install (#63)

* fix(copilot-cli): remove conflicting gh alias before extension install

gh extension install fails with '"copilot" matches the name of a
built-in command or alias' when a prior partial install left a gh
alias named copilot.

Fix: check gh alias list and delete any conflicting copilot alias
before attempting the extension install.

Also removed the gh copilot --version post-install check, which
had the same conflict potential and routed stdout errors into the
log_ok output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(squad): Merge decision inbox - Guard gh alias conflicts

- Added decision: Guard against gh alias conflicts before gh extension install
- PR #63 (copilot-cli fix) — check for conflicting alias, remove --version check
- Merged inbox/donald-copilot-alias-fix.md to decisions.md
- Updated Donald's agent history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(copilot-cli): handle gh built-in promotion in 2.x+

gh 2.89.0 promotes 'gh copilot' to a built-in command. The previous
fix checked gh alias list, which doesn't catch built-ins.

Two-part fix:
1. Idempotency check now uses 'gh copilot --help' — works for
   built-ins, extensions, and aliases.
2. Wrap install attempt with set +e to capture the "matches built-in"
   error and exit gracefully instead of failing the whole setup.

Alias guard retained as defensive code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(squad): log gh built-in promotion learning to donald history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(copilot-cli): use 'gh copilot -- --help' for idempotency check

On gh 2.89.0+, 'gh copilot --help' exits 0 even before the Copilot
CLI binary has been downloaded — it shows gh's own wrapper help and
never touches the binary. This caused the idempotency check to always
short-circuit to 'already available' on a fresh system.

Fix: use 'gh copilot -- --help' instead. The '--' passes the flag
through to the actual Copilot CLI binary, so:
  - binary already present → exits 0 immediately (idempotent)
  - binary not yet downloaded → triggers proactive download, then exits 0
  - older gh without extension → exits non-zero → falls through to install

Also update the file-header comment to document this nuance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(squad): append idempotency passthrough fix to donald history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dotfiles): append managed block to existing .zshrc/.bashrc (#64) (#65)

* fix(dotfiles): append managed block to existing .zshrc/.bashrc (#64)

- Add append_managed_block() helper: checks for marker before appending
- .zshrc: append managed block when file exists; copy template on fresh install
- .bashrc: append managed block when file exists (new — was previously untouched)
- Managed block sets PATH for uv/local bins, inits nvm, sources .aliases
- .bashrc block omits nvm init (nvm installer already appends it there)
- Idempotent: marker '# --- dev-setup managed block' guards re-runs
- --dry-run mode reports what would happen without writing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(squad): pluto history for issue #64 managed block

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(gitattributes): add eol=lf rules for shell scripts (#66)

Add text=auto normalization and explicit eol=lf for *.sh and *.bash
to prevent CRLF line-ending failures when scripts are volume-mounted
into Devcontainers from a Windows host.

Reviewed and merged PR #66 (fix .gitattributes eol=lf for shell scripts).
Critical fix for Windows Devcontainer CRLF issue. Closed issue #67.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(squad): merge decision inbox, log crlf gitattributes fix

- Merged .squad/decisions/inbox/pluto-dotfiles-managed-block.md into decisions.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(donald): log PRs #70 and #71 — issues #68 and #69

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(shell): merge stderr into stdout for ordered output in piped environments (#68) (#70)

Add \xec 2>&1\ immediately after \set -euo pipefail\ in both
setup.sh and scripts/linux/setup.sh. This merges stderr into stdout
so log_error() output appears in-sequence with all other log output
when running inside a Devcontainer or piped environment.

The two tool scripts in scripts/linux/tools/ have no >&2 calls
and inherit the merged file descriptor from the parent — no changes
needed there.

Fixes #68

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(devcontainer): strip CRLF from .sh files on container create (#69) (#71)

Add onCreateCommand to strip carriage returns from all shell scripts
before postCreateCommand runs. This guards against the case where a
Windows user's working tree still has CRLF line endings after the
PR #66 gitattributes fix, which updates git's index but NOT the
working tree.

  find . -name '*.sh' | xargs sed -i 's/\r//'

- Safe on Linux: no-op if files are already LF
- Defensive against future CRLF drift from any Windows checkout
- No-op in GitHub Codespaces (fresh clone is always LF)
- Runs BEFORE postCreateCommand so bash setup.sh never sees \r

Fixes #69

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(squad): Issues #68–#69 fixed (stdout/stderr merge + CRLF guard)

gh 2.89.0+ skips the install prompt when IsCI() returns true (CI env var
set). Replaces the unreliable script(1) PTY approach with a single env var
that triggers a non-interactive binary download.

Binary is stored as ~/.local/share/gh/copilot/copilot ("copilot", not
"gh-copilot"). Updated comment to reflect this.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(jetson): harden setup script against all failure modes

- Step 6: Add curl to system packages (needed for model download in step 9)
- Step 8: Create config.json if missing (no repo file AND no example)
  Wrap provisioning in set +e so failure doesn't abort entire setup
- Step 9: Use CFG_SUPA_URL/KEY to avoid env var collision; remove hard
  exit 1 for missing config — warn and continue instead
  Add 2>/dev/null to curl/stat to prevent set -e crash
- Step 10: Check trtexec exists before attempting conversion; wrap in
  set +e so conversion failure doesn't abort setup
- Step 12: Check /usr/share/applications exists before creating shortcut
  (headless Jetson may not have desktop directory)

All non-blocking failures now warn and continue instead of aborting.
Only 2 hard exits remain: non-ARM64 abort and missing Docker.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Upgrade HTTP endpoints to HTTPS in Azure App Service (#16060)

* Upgrade HTTP endpoints to HTTPS in Azure App Service

HTTP endpoints are now upgraded to HTTPS:443 by default in App Service,
matching the existing behavior in Azure Container Apps. App Service already
redirects HTTP to HTTPS at the platform level, so this ensures connection
strings reflect the actual serving scheme.

- Add PreserveHttpEndpoints property to AzureAppServiceEnvironmentResource
- Add WithHttpsUpgrade() extension method to opt out
- Upgrade HTTP->HTTPS in ProcessEndpoints with consolidated logging
- Add CanPreserveHttpSchemeUsingWithHttpsUpgrade test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add remarks and example to WithHttpsUpgrade XML docs

Mirror the ACA WithHttpsUpgrade documentation pattern with remarks
explaining opt-out behavior and a usage example.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CanPreserveHttpSchemeUsingWithHttpsUpgrade test to verify endpoint URLs

Add a second project that references project1 so the snapshot captures
the resolved endpoint URLs (services__project1__http__0). This proves
WithHttpsUpgrade(false) actually preserves http:// scheme instead of
upgrading to https://.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Metaculus: use continuous_range quantile array for P(date < cutoff)

The v3 API returns possibilities.continuous_range: a 201-point array of ISO
date strings representing the inverse CDF (quantile function) of the community
forecast. Replaced broken aggregations-based approach with binary search +
interpolation on this array.

commit.message:
feat(plugin): add Copilot CLI plugin support

Add native `marketplace.json` and `plugin/plugin.json` manifests so `copilot plugin marketplace add` and `copilot plugin install` work without relying on Claude-specific fallback paths. Wire Copilot session hooks through `plugin/hooks/copilot.json` and document the shared plugin flow in `README.md`, `plugin/README.md`, and `cmd/auto.go`.

Keep `Claude Code` on `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` and `plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` so both CLIs can share the `plugin/` package without sharing incompatible hook schemas. Update `release-please-config.json` so plugin version bumps keep the Copilot manifest in sync.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Manuel Martinez <manmartgarc@gmail.com>

DeepAnalysisModal.tsx into src/components/DeepAnalysisModal/:

- ModalHeader: stock name, badges, quick stats panel

overview kept inline in main file as they depend on getRadarData.

commit.message:
Simplify WorkoutSetEditor: remove predictions, tissue checks, status, timestamps

Remove from the log-mode session editor:
- Struck-through model predictions (prescription banner)
- Tissue feedback/checks section
- Rep completion status (full/partial/failed) dropdown
- Completed-at timestamp input and started/finished display
- Recent sets indicator (scheme history summary)

Keep:
- RPE input, weight/reps/duration/distance inputs, side selector
- Exercise and set deletion
- F1 style lights in RecentSessionsCard
- Total volume and exercise count summary
- Add set / add exercise functionality

Also removes dead code: prescription fetching, tissue readiness state,
completion detection, and related helper functions/imports.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: diet-tracker
repository.description: diet tracker

commit.message:
Authentic Japanese design overhaul

Redesign the entire UI with authentic Japanese design principles:

- Ma (): Generous whitespace, breathing room in all layouts
- Kanso (): Simplified UI, removed emoji icons, cleaner typography
- Shibui (): Understated elegance with subtle, refined details
- Wabi-sabi (): Natural, imperfect beauty in the design language

Color palette: Traditional Japanese  ai indigo (#2C4A7C),colors
kinari warm white (#F7F4EF), sumi ink (#1A1A2E), wakakusa green,
urushi lacquer red. All semantic status colors updated.

Typography: Added Noto Serif JP for headings and Japanese text,
paired with Geist Sans for body. Bilingual micro-labels throughout.

Layout: Asymmetric grid compositions, editorial-style sections,
vertical dividers, sticky side navigation. Moved from centered
marketing-card patterns to editorial Japanese product feel.

Components: Refined header with roofline mark, seigaiha wave
pattern footer, softer card borders, ichimoji dividers, gentle
300ms+ transitions, traditional gradient placeholders.

Inspired by Muji, Monocle Japan, and high-end Japanese real estate.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Kenai Network escrow & direct-sale transaction platform

- Unified escrow schema (transactions, events, documents, messages, trust scores, reviews)
- Stripe Connect escrow flow (fund → inspect → conditions → release)
- AI Transaction Advisor (local Qwen LLM on port 8095)
- Direct-sale tools (disclosure checklists, closing cost estimator, title tracker, purchase agreements)
- Fee savings calculator (1% escrow vs 5-6% traditional agent commission)
- Network trust badges and verified seller profiles
- Escrow guarantee security banners
- Admin dashboard with dispute resolution and platform config
- Full RLS policies and immutable audit logging
- Demo mode for all components when Supabase is offline

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Scroll to top when advancing to next group in results reveal (#23)

When revealing results group-by-group, the page position wasn't reset
between groups, leaving users scrolled mid-page after advancing.

## Change

- `results-reveal.tsx`: call `window.scrollTo(0, 0)` alongside the state
increment in the "Reveal next group" button's `onClick`

```tsx
onClick={() => { setRevealedCount((c) => c + 1); window.scrollTo(0, 0); }}
```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: valscion <482561+valscion@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: extract QueriesUserAccounts trait and FinAccountLot::computeMetrics (#209)

* refactor: extract QueriesUserAccounts trait and FinAccountLot::computeMetrics

Eliminates ~20 copies of the inline account-ownership check and the
repeated date-diff / gain-loss computation spread across the three
finance controllers.

- New `QueriesUserAccounts` trait (`Concerns/`) with `resolveOwnedAccount`
  and `getUserAccountIds` — applied to FinanceAccountsController,
  FinanceLotsController, and FinanceTransactionsApiController
- New `FinAccountLot::computeMetrics()` static method replaces duplicate
  purchase/sale date-diff and realized-gain-loss calculations in
  importLots and saveAnalyzedLots
- Removes all direct Auth::id() / FinAccounts::where('acct_owner') calls
  from the three controllers; each now delegates through the trait

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: finance:k1-migrate command + normalising parsed_data accessor (closes #204)

- New `finance:k1-migrate` artisan command backfills legacy flat-format K-1
  records to the canonical schemaVersion structure using K1LegacyTransformer.
  Supports --dry-run; idempotent (skips already-canonical records); scoped to
  FINANCE_CLI_USER_ID.

- Adds a normalising `parsedData` Eloquent Attribute accessor to
  FileForTaxDocument: on read, legacy K-1 parsed_data is transparently
  transformed to the canonical shape, so all consumers (MCP tools,
  future finance:tax-render) always see a single schema without branching.
  The setter JSON-encodes on write; the raw DB value is unchanged until the
  backfill command runs.

- Removes 'parsed_data' from $casts (superseded by the custom Attribute).

- 7 new feature tests covering migration, dry-run, idempotency, user scoping,
  and the model accessor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address all PR review comments - type safety, query scope, skipped counters, env cleanup

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bherila/2025-website/sessions/5b4fe334-c082-4eef-87a7-983a3efe789c

Co-authored-by: bherila <1422004+bherila@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: ensure fresh clone setup works end-to-end

- sync_env.py: Create config.json from config.example.json if missing
  (was silently skipping on fresh clone since config.json is .gitignored)
- setup_jetson.sh: Move provisioning to step 8 (before model download)
  so Supabase credentials are available for ONNX model download in step 9.
  Support 3 credential sources: provisioning token, manual entry, env vars.
- setup_windows_dev.bat: Always run sync_env.py regardless of .env source
  (was skipping when .env.example was used)
- Update jetson_deployment_guide.md to reflect new step ordering

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
